behavior_summary: U8P_STRAW::look is the object's examine (U8P_STRAW::look) handler. When the player examines the STRAW actor/instance, the process emits a single bark line containing the text "straw " and then immediately suspends/returns.
There is no branching, menu interaction, flag read/write, or external function calls in this snippet; the behavior is a simple player-facing description/bark.
The function is classified as a U8P_STRAW::look-type process (process_type 0x0208), so it is invoked by the engine's 'U8P_STRAW::look/examine' interaction for that actor or object.
notable_control_flow: Entry: invoked as a 'U8P_STRAW::look' process (examine event) on the STRAW actor.
Exit: suspend/terminate the process immediately after the bark; no further logic or state changes.
open_questions: Is the trailing space in the bark text "straw " meaningful (concatenation placeholder) or an artifact of extraction?
Does the U8P_STRAW::look handler ever get called from other engine events (fast-area entry/leave) in other contexts, or strictly from player examine?
Are there related resources (voice, subtitle flags, or alternate descriptions) linked to this actor elsewhere in the project that would change how the bark is presented?

main_function.name: U8P_STRAW::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description / examination response for the STRAW actor by playing a single bark text and ending the process.
entry_conditions: Triggered by a 'U8P_STRAW::look' (examine) event on the STRAW actor (process type 0x0208).
Likely invoked when the player inspects or targets the straw object/area in the game world.

function_map.name: U8P_STRAW::look
function_map.role: Examine/U8P_STRAW::look handler that emits a single bark line and then suspends.
